differential_diagnoses: Metastatic malignancy causing myelophthisis (occult primary, possibly lung or pancreas)
Disseminated intravascular coagulation secondary to malignancy
Tuberculosis or other granulomatous marrow infiltration
next_steps: Bone marrow aspiration and biopsy with cytogenetics and flow cytometry
Comprehensive oncologic workup including PET/CT and markers for occult malignancy
Evaluation for secondary HLH using HScore and soluble IL-2 receptor level
Supportive care with blood product transfusions as needed for symptomatic anemia/thrombocytopenia
Management of acute pancreatitis and supportive analgesic care
Early goals-of-care discussions in the context of progressive multisystem involvement
